zh.lifeSummary: 當Ann Pelverton於1505年出生在英國英格蘭賀福爾時，她的父親Sir Thomas Hoo Esq, VII40歲，母親Lady Ann Mary Newman Palgrove25歲。她於1516年與Sir Thomas Pitway Reade Reed在英國英格蘭波克夏拉德利結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1575年10月30日在英國波克夏阿炳頓聖 倫恩斯 邱奇享年70歲。

nameMeanings.name: Pelverton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Devon): habitational name from either of two places called Yelverton, in Norfolk or Devon. Medieval examples of the surname are from the Norfolk place, but the modern surname in Britain is associated mainly with Devon, where the earliest record of the surname is found in 1761, and is probably a substitution for Elford , an earlier form of the Devon placename. The Devon place, a hamlet in Buckland Monachorum, is not officially on record as Yelverton until the 19th century, when the Great Western Railway so named the station it built there in 1859. It is a dialect pronunciation of Elverton, which is first recorded in 1765 as an extended form of its medieval name Elford + -ton ‘town’. The Norfolk placename is recorded as Ailuertuna in 1086, and probably derives from the Old English personal name Æthelfrith or Geldfrith + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Elford
meanings: English: habitational name from Yelverton in Buckland Monachorum (Devon), recorded as Elleford in 1291 (see Yelverton ), named from the Old English personal name Ella or elle, ellen ‘elder tree’ + ford ‘ford’. The name may also derive from any of the places called Elford in Staffordshire and Northumberland, both probably from the Old English personal name Ella + ford.

variants.name: Yelvington
meanings: Probably an altered form of English   Yelverton  .

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Ann Pelverton was born in 1505, in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Thomas Hoo Esq, VII, was 40 and her mother, Lady Ann Mary Newman Palgrove, was 25. She married Sir Thomas Pitway Reade Reed in 1516, in Radley, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died on 30 October 1575, in St Helen's Church, Abingdon, Berkshire, England, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ann Pelverton est née en 1505, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Thomas Hoo Esq, VII, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Lady Ann Mary Newman Palgrove, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Pitway Reade Reed en 1516, à Radley, Berkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Она умерла 30 октября 1575 г. в местности ст хелен'с чурч, абингдон, биркшир, Англия в возрасте 70 лет.
